Goals coaching

Bring your goals to life.

Turn your passions into plans with free, one-on-one guidance from a U.S. Bank goals coach.

Meet with a coach Explore your goals

Collaborative, purposeful guidance

We believe in human potential. The power to change “I wish” to “I will” to “I did.” The power that comes from envisioning your future, even if the journey feels unclear.

That’s why U.S. Bank offers free goals coaching. A coach can help you explore what matters most to you, financial or not, and create a customized success strategy. Come prepared for a supportive, stress-free conversation about who you are, what you hope to achieve and clear steps you can take to build the life you want.

Explore your goals.

Your coach helps pull your true self forward as you explore the goals most important to you.

Make an action plan.

You'll partner with your coach to identify realistic, meaningful steps towards goal achievement.

Gain connections and support.

Some goals may require more help to achieve. Your coach can connect you to the right people.

Real goals brought to life

See what clients are saying about their goals coaching experience.

Founded a nonprofit

‟When my photography business slowed down during the pandemic, my coach asked the right questions and helped me figure out the road to take to reinvent myself.”

Kim

 

Bought their first home

‟My coach is my accountability partner. We were on a two-year plan to save a down payment, and she helped us speed up the process.” (Corey is a U.S. Bank employee.)

Corey and Stephenie

 

Started a new career

‟Working with my goals coach has allowed me to see how setting goals works and why they’re important. I didn’t think it was possible to make these changes over the course of a year. My goals coach understood me. He didn’t have an agenda. He challenged me to get clear about my goals and be assertive with myself about what I wanted to achieve.”

Justin

 

What our goals coaching clients say

Results are based on a survey conducted by U.S. Bank of more than 400 goals coaching clients from 2019 through 2023.

96%

of participants said it was worth their time.

94%

of participants felt more motivated to pursue their goals.

93%

of participants said their goals coach made them feel special.

Why is coaching free?

We believe life guidance should be available to anyone who wants it. What’s in it for the bank? Our goals coaches can’t sell you anything. But our hope is as you achieve your goals, you’ll invest more in yourself via our products.

Get answers to more of the most frequently asked questions about goals coaching.

Meet our coaches.

With backgrounds in fields such as psychology and education, our coaches can help you achieve what matters most to you, whether that’s sticking to a budget, building your career or pursuing a passion.

Ready to start your journey? Use our interactive calendar to schedule a virtual session with a coach.

Watch our recorded goals webinars.

Along with free coaching, we also offer webinars on goals-related topics. Recordings are available for you to watch whenever it’s convenient.

View the webinars

Frequently asked questions

Coaching is for anyone looking for life guidance and advice. It’s for anyone who’s ever thought about goal-setting and planning but didn’t know where to start or hasn’t done much about it.

Maybe you’re looking to save for college. Or, you may just be starting out in your career. Perhaps you and your partner want to buy a house or need help planning for retirement. Whatever you’re looking for, you have a personal connection to an expert and can get the help you need.

Goals coaches are neither bankers nor financial advisors. With backgrounds in psychology, behavioral science, education or related fields (and in some cases, financial services), coaches are professionals who bring experience to different life stages and are trained in goal achievement methodologies, helping you focus on positive habit change toward your goal. Whether you want to manage debt or save for the future (including retirement), they can help you set milestones and events so you can see your progress. Plus, you get that human touch to help you stay motivated.

Coaching sessions provide access to professional and personalized advice for you for free. Our coaches help you learn how to achieve your personal finance goals so you can apply insights toward achieving more goals into the future. If you find while exploring your goals that you’d like to talk with a financial planner or advisor, your coach can help you connect with one.

You can meet with a coach solo or bring someone special. This session is a space free from judgment to ask questions and get answers in real time. You and your coach will spend an hour, virtually, identifying goals important to you and when you want to reach each of them. You’ll organize and place these goals on your timeline. This will give you a greater understanding of how your goals relate to each other so you can take the necessary steps toward greater financial wellness and, ultimately, your best life.

No, there are no requirements but you should have a general idea of your current financial situation. Whether or not you already have your goals in mind, find the right coach for you and either schedule a 15-minute call to learn more or go ahead and book your 60-minute coaching session online.

Yes. After your session, you’ll receive a goals discovery session summary. In the second session, you and your coach will collaborate to create an action plan to help you stay focused and on track to achieve your goals. Some goals may require more help to achieve and your coach can connect you to the right people. If you choose, you can have an ongoing, supportive relationship in attaining your goals.

You can see availability for any coach you choose when you use our online booking tool. When you schedule a meeting, a calendar will open with available times for a 15-minute call or a 60-minute session and you select the one that works best for you.

Start of disclosure content

Disclosures

Goals coaches do not:

  • Conduct financial planning or provide investment advice.
  • Make recommendations or give advice on matters involving health, including physical, mental, emotional or medical.

U.S. Bank assumes no responsibility for and makes no claims concerning the merit or sufficiency of your goals and does not assume any responsibility or liability for any losses or other outcomes resulting from decisions made by you, actions taken or not taken by you, in connection with U.S. Bank and U.S. Bank Goals Coaching services.

The following U.S. Bank communications, and documents related to your contractual agreements, disclosures, notices, and statements, Internet and mobile banking services may only be available in English. You must be able to read and understand these documents, or have assistance in translating them, in order to understand and use this product or service. English documents available upon request..

Member FDIC.
